free_text: Excitation functions for production of heavy
actinides from interactions of 40Ca and 48Ca ions
with 248Cm
The same
radiations and abundances and techniques were used.
Two different targets containing 1.5 and 1.7 mg/cm2
of 248Cm in the form of Cm203, prepared by stepwise
electrodeposition with a diameter of 9 mm on 2.4
mg/cm2 Be foil, were used.
Irradiations of 248Cm with 48Ca ions
were performed at the SuperHILAC at LBL with incident
energies on target of 239, 263, and 288 MeV and at
the UNILAC at the GSI with 263 and 318 MeV.
Irradiations with 234-, 259-, and 294-MeV 40Ca ions
were also conducted at the UNILAC. The copper catcher
foils were dissolved in concentrated HC1 containing a
few drops of HNO3 and the actinides were
co-precipitated on ferric hydroxide by the addition
of ammonia to complex the copper, leaving it in
solution. The final fractions were evaporated to
dryness and the resulting samples were counted for
alpha, spontaneous fission, and gamma activities, and
data analysis was performed as described before.
The standard deviation associated with the
quoted absolute cross sections is estimated to be 12%.
Corrections have been made for the total measured
energy losses of about 75 MeV for Ca-40 and 97 MeV for
Ca-48 ions prior to entry into the Cm target. Energy
losses in the target were 13 to 16 MeV and the energy
range is indicated in the tables.

Chemical yields were measured to be 11%,27%, and 21%
for the 234-, 259-, and 294-MeV bombardments,
respectively.
The statistical standard deviation s given in
the table, which is based on the analysis of the
decay data.

892-keV gamma ray.

An intensity of 0.40 was used for the measured
379-keV gamma ray.
Only the 8.6-h isomer was measured and a ratio
of 0.5 for the yield of metastable to ground state
isomers was used to calculate total cross section.
